record.field: Kener is a modern, lightweight self-hosted status page that turns service health checks into a polished public uptime and incident-communication site.

Kener is a modern, lightweight self-hosted status page that turns service health checks into a polished public uptime and incident-communication site.

Built with SvelteKit and Node.js, Kener probes your services with multiple monitor types and publishes their state on a clean, brandable status page. It pairs uptime tracking with full incident management and scheduled maintenance windows, so teams can keep users informed when things go wrong. Monitors and parsing logic are configured in YAML, letting you define exactly what counts as up, down, or degraded.

Key features
  • Many monitor types: HTTP/API, Ping, TCP, DNS, SSL, SQL, heartbeat and more
  • Incident management and scheduled maintenance with public announcements
  • Notifications via Email, Slack, Discord and webhooks
  • Customizable, brandable UI with multi-language support
  • YAML-based monitor configuration with custom up/down/degraded parsing
  • REST API, embeddable widgets and role-based access for teams
Best for

Teams and operators who want a simple, great-looking public status page to communicate uptime and incidents without the cost or complexity of enterprise platforms like Atlassian Statuspage.

services: kener: image: rajnandan1/kener:latest environment: - TZ={{ tz }} - KENER_SECRET_KEY={{ kener_secret_key }} - DATABASE_URL={{ database_url }} - KENER_BASE_PATH={{ kener_base_path }} - ORIGIN={{ origin }} - RESEND_API_KEY={{ resend_api_key }} - RESEND_SENDER_EMAIL={{ resend_sender_email }} ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3000' volumes: - ./kener_db:/app/database - ../files/uploads:/app/uploads restart: unless-stopped container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network postgres: image: postgres:alpine environment: - POSTGRES_USER={{ postgres_user }} - POSTGRES_PASSWORD={{ postgres_password }} - POSTGRES_DB={{ postgres_db }} restart: unless-stopped container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgres' networks: - internal mysql: image: mariadb:11 environment: - MYSQL_USER={{ mysql_user }} - MYSQL_PASSWORD={{ mysql_password }} - MYSQL_DATABASE={{ mysql_database }} - MYSQL_RANDOM_ROOT_PASSWORD=true restart: unless-stopped container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_mysql' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
